name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/7dc9fd2546da1accf6e5e0e6fba22172
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/d6f045ccd040889d1a975d071fe8a68a
location: Root cause for this ANR is unknown

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/accb102cf70d5aacb234d85bcf409820
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/2508e2ae81cbc84f2269339a2da9988e
location: Root cause for this ANR is unknown

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/9948a1fd83bc2d36532c705ad7229c4e
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/2c7b8f9a880cfe6cfb2747d1a7d33025
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/6e6a042b638b7ce50d5e100e53346c9e
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in main thread

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/4afceb0ab617d8aa7773b518e7723e25
location: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError

annotations.title: Out of memory
annotations.body:
These crashes occur when your app attempts to allocate more memory than what's available in the Android system. This typically happens when an app leaks memory or uses large assets inefficiently, such as images or videos.

There are a few ways you can optimize memory in your app to help avoid out of memory crashes. Try using smaller assets, release memory when your app enters the background, and / or debug potential memory leaks. (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/androidgo/optimize-memory)

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

location: Input dispatching timed out (No focused window)

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/5db43cba8a099bc7a5fcc29062ec4899
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/5a9a24f3a2c2871270aded3a45f47b0a
location: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError

annotations.title: Out of memory
annotations.body:
These crashes occur when your app attempts to allocate more memory than what's available in the Android system. This typically happens when an app leaks memory or uses large assets inefficiently, such as images or videos.

There are a few ways you can optimize memory in your app to help avoid out of memory crashes. Try using smaller assets, release memory when your app enters the background, and / or debug potential memory leaks. (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/androidgo/optimize-memory)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/6572cfac7188c0d50ad02c0d2b5687a3
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in blamed thread

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/aef1b9433da3183c1765a38bd1fe215c
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/720e1ef5df8a5be2bac80d60303c9ef9
location: Root cause for this ANR is unknown

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/fdb2c74b0ebf84113cfbfa0757382433
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/22a44a7c993713e573a80c409c1064a2
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/38794c4eaabd8e159122bf59ce189b43
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/e90f8b5c2103016218257fa4915c1957

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/231ef61d401c679f6be4476ad4c51363
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/1ed266c8283892281d0c73ad32c383d6
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/40e9d3137d14ca3b303ca87d74141e32
location: Input dispatching timed out

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/cee310299134158e64faca84b57fc6a9
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/e426581e9416ece5db580673585c6611
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in main thread

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/4670a11d3fd65885c3443aecb6257906
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/7accea69f4f22acd5bbe1d759ff9400c
location: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError

annotations.title: Out of memory
annotations.body:
These crashes occur when your app attempts to allocate more memory than what's available in the Android system. This typically happens when an app leaks memory or uses large assets inefficiently, such as images or videos.

There are a few ways you can optimize memory in your app to help avoid out of memory crashes. Try using smaller assets, release memory when your app enters the background, and / or debug potential memory leaks. (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/androidgo/optimize-memory)

location: ANR triggered by main thread being blocked

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/a6de15e9a91794d45b55e1a9282d0460
location: Input dispatching timed out

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/1613b9ff7d0c427bc854f858f1643094
location: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError

annotations.title: Out of memory
annotations.body:
These crashes occur when your app attempts to allocate more memory than what's available in the Android system. This typically happens when an app leaks memory or uses large assets inefficiently, such as images or videos.

There are a few ways you can optimize memory in your app to help avoid out of memory crashes. Try using smaller assets, release memory when your app enters the background, and / or debug potential memory leaks. (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/androidgo/optimize-memory)

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/a65b3e975ba64639bb5b8adab38ada84
location: Broadcast of Intent { act=android.intent.action.BOOT_COMPLETED cmp=com.microsoft.scmx/com.instacart.library.truetime.BootCompletedBroadcastReceiver }

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/e7b6b466f1c2a9b1edb513ad830defc6
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in blamed thread

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/578b817fa97e547b9faf5d3ce990f22f
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/f14bdc0fa9197535e1b5e2b71cefbf8e
location: Input dispatching timed out (No focused window)

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/fc718a2a4b0709a7c2e8a919acf4c46b
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

location: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError

annotations.title: Out of memory
annotations.body:
These crashes occur when your app attempts to allocate more memory than what's available in the Android system. This typically happens when an app leaks memory or uses large assets inefficiently, such as images or videos.

There are a few ways you can optimize memory in your app to help avoid out of memory crashes. Try using smaller assets, release memory when your app enters the background, and / or debug potential memory leaks. (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/androidgo/optimize-memory)

You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/c12bf0f34a98959d5b8e5c6594455677
location: Root cause for this ANR is unknown

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/ef571e78c474c15e336826c6f9320787
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/f95c38c02cff275e5e53114da5757e4c
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in main thread

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/45563326d1393d983279274faab865bb
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

annotations.title: Out of memory
annotations.body:
These crashes occur when your app attempts to allocate more memory than what's available in the Android system. This typically happens when an app leaks memory or uses large assets inefficiently, such as images or videos.

There are a few ways you can optimize memory in your app to help avoid out of memory crashes. Try using smaller assets, release memory when your app enters the background, and / or debug potential memory leaks. (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/androidgo/optimize-memory)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/217321410b2a0c2745932846befc379a
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/49002cd121cd96ad3f91bc5c95745287
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/5def86259c4c364b29a2e63ed4125a55
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/7189d1b99fa50d1ff5cd752fdb30152e
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/957a69c5226d93a608d646043882498d
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/97b335e1633d105a2ff3c9643875fb95
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/a9ced10ff2259da1adaf6b5b33efae0b
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in blamed thread

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/b36071b4b7b837a70b11ba000e2a6d30
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.tokenshare.TokenSharingService

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/c58c68d7286475f462b7b0238fba58a1
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in main thread

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/ce29b8aa05b5860042d71bada85bd60d
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/.vpn.VpnServiceOrchestrator

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/0c7db5b3c9e79b73d7f21e5246309af1
location: SIGSEGV

annotations.title: Non-fatal memory error
annotations.body:
This non-fatal memory error did not immediately cause your app to crash, but it may lead to crashes or other faulty behavior in the future
Non-fatal memory errors come with additional information to help you understand this type of issue. Details about how to debug this issue, and information about where memory has been allocated and freed up, can be found in the backtrace section of the stack trace.

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/0dd92c0deb4513a6d928c4d744a3b96a
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/0f00755c61d8a5565c60f133d1a1c93e
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in blamed thread

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/202293ca30aa5c65c7f9a0f65a9f6d67
location: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError

annotations.title: Out of memory
annotations.body:
These crashes occur when your app attempts to allocate more memory than what's available in the Android system. This typically happens when an app leaks memory or uses large assets inefficiently, such as images or videos.

There are a few ways you can optimize memory in your app to help avoid out of memory crashes. Try using smaller assets, release memory when your app enters the background, and / or debug potential memory leaks. (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/androidgo/optimize-memory)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/20b3a3c66308764ecef908c7062afacb
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/229824112319d5bdbf28408669b612ad
location: Root cause for this ANR is unknown

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/2a144a20e2167babc365d83bd4a08a52
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/2e2760ce48c8073c7176ebb8cb2ce670
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/36950452ad297ff0deba09eac14a32a7
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/4f6d84416a5aeb2caecc6ea279d69d9b
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/6fa971621461e184663f9da4514b4ba7
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

cause: [libc.so] __futex_wait_ex(void volatile*, bool, int, bool, timespec const*)
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in main thread

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/96403210a189fa06c7b7ed196aaf8d72
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/a1a338606435de6dbb3700ead1204684
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in blamed thread

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/ae6250558bea1610401490121b11d92a
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)
