location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/cae91529d9429eb7021e2f783b31d0d0
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/cd9bae73e49e6ca4672ecaa9c90b761f
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityServicenull

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/f61841ccbee59d188ae8d1cfcfa1ea0b
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/fdb42a5c47cd5f2170e7a599d5627cfa
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/fe19441112c7ada1f2115c2feed34f09
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/0a6ba0b651a3fece1ce31d1799049c06
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/15d37ad4d3fde22b5b29289813073bae
location: Input dispatching timed out (No focused window)

annotations.title: Unresponsive GPU
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting for the rendering subsystem or the GPU to complete a requested operation. This is usually caused by the slowness of the rendering subsystem, the GPU, or its driver.

These ANRs are a source of jank, and can be caused by your app sending too much work, or  bugs in the system, kernel, or graphics drivers. These ANRs tend to be device-dependent, and difficult to action without a system trace. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#input-dispatch-common-causes)

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/16ea5db17741f96eb3e484037f54db65
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/19aa0c2d426fd846c8c6825073a55eb1
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/19c554e0130a5e2012728b188087fefa
location: ANR triggered by main thread being blocked

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/34bc45b4e66d176a08e9eb93102c4519
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in main thread

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/34f60cc31706090e7cae89b26e9738ed
location: java.lang.UnsatisfiedLinkError

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/3515e9e84ea74b4dd0a1bd09fa4d1e2e
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.tokenshare.TokenSharingService

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/351b9c3ae1934173f8edebf36753bab9
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

location: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError

annotations.title: Out of memory
annotations.body:
These crashes occur when your app attempts to allocate more memory than what's available in the Android system. This typically happens when an app leaks memory or uses large assets inefficiently, such as images or videos.

There are a few ways you can optimize memory in your app to help avoid out of memory crashes. Try using smaller assets, release memory when your app enters the background, and / or debug potential memory leaks. (https://developer.android.com/guide/topics/androidgo/optimize-memory)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/36108bbe719f6c48e03c31110f4a845d
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/37769accb3538dc604c1f3135a92f2cc
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in blamed thread

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/379dc106a460f97fb053a4782e0a4296
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/4da0863ed3b05fe4be5fed27a205112e
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/4e788e08b6be89357a1eda028844cfc7
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/66c30a5d5cdecf3439237a9dac0c9433
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/6846954e65c19a66141e4da14fb5ec5c
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/6cd9e2be842c881fa60497dba1ab355f
location: ANR triggered by slow operations in blamed thread

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/6e798c2aa012449cce0556a270d090d7
location: Input dispatching timed out

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/7325df7c69fb9122dc66936c508d0dfa
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/745c38854eb68487f0b79ec96bf3e85c
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/82df7f88e199997d9301cf2b849262a9
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/85510eaa445cba2f39be5aadd221bfc4
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/8f88c0b8db4d8b91588add05b4c83229
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/902ce21277110dcf19a49c98afb9f475
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/94f70611b536e8d7b6c031623318efe9
location: ANR triggered by main thread being blocked

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/a2273e3c2fe9d179c845d99234ecc44e
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/a2ac191dbb26c8e66c137005764f5e39
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemjob.SystemJobService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/a7aad39714f6c26212efe4557077c602
location: ANR triggered by main thread being blocked

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

annotations.title: Native lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting on a native synchronization routine, such as a mutex.

Native synchronization routines don't provide details on the exact lock, or where it is being held. Find the locked mutex in your source, and then locate other code locations where it is being acquired. You can use Android Studio's profiler to detect potential lock contentions if multiple threads frequently compete for the same lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/b3da17599986f36ff59139a82b4438a8
location: Executing service com.microsoft.scmx/com.microsoft.defender.antiphishing.accessibility.BrowserAccessibilityService

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/b3e2020686c8ae94c84a82ecf4e819ce
location: Input dispatching timed out (No focused window)

annotations.title: Unresponsive GPU
annotations.body:
The main thread is blocked, waiting for the rendering subsystem or the GPU to complete a requested operation. This is usually caused by the slowness of the rendering subsystem, the GPU, or its driver.

These ANRs are a source of jank, and can be caused by your app sending too much work, or  bugs in the system, kernel, or graphics drivers. These ANRs tend to be device-dependent, and difficult to action without a system trace. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#input-dispatch-common-causes)

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/bee3b7d9180efde4f0b55aabf80247aa
location: Input dispatching timed out

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/c014c450b8fc210edc0019cae90cf5a4
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: Lock contention
annotations.body:
The main thread was blocked trying to acquire a lock that was held by another thread.

Try to minimize the use of locks in the main thread, and make sure that whoever holds those locks does not hold them for long. Avoid expensive and unpredictable operations like I/O or Binder calls while holding a lock. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#lock-contention)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/ccd1979b95f44cec294bb5048524946e
location: ANR triggered by thread waiting for a binder transaction

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/cd703b97fa3a47fb3480385d55f7869c
location: Broadcast of Intent { act=android.intent.action.TIMEZONE_CHANGED cmp=com.microsoft.scmx/androidx.work.impl.background.systemalarm.RescheduleReceiver }

annotations.title: Main thread idle
annotations.body:
These issues are not actionable as the stack traces do not show the blocking condition that is causing the ANR.

This type of ANR report is produced when the ANR condition has passed by the time the stack trace is captured. This is usually caused by high load or resource contention inside the Android system server, which may contribute to the root cause of the ANR.

It is not possible to determine the root cause of ANRs where the main thread is idle. These ANRs are included in Android vitals for completeness, as they help to show the number of users affected by ANRs. To fix the underlying root cause, you should look at ANRs with the same type and activity, but with different main thread states. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#nativepollonce)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/cdc56097dcb1782b067edfa08096543a
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

annotations.title: Slow app startup
annotations.body:
An ANR was detected during the app startup process. Slow operation during startup can result in ANRs and may be the reason behind this issue.
Improving startup latency can reduce the occurrences of ANRs. Avoid expensive or blocking operations, such as I/O, which can delay the startup process and result in these ANRs. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/diagnose-and-fix-anrs#example-slow)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/daaf38ecc04fd2730d422adf7d27fa2c
location: ANR triggered by slow IO operations

annotations.title: I/O in main thread
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy performing an I/O operation. This could be caused by network or file access (especially compressed files), but can also be caused by operations such as ClassLoader.loadClass.

I/O operations are very unpredictable, and can block a thread for a long time. Avoid I/O in the main thread and refactor your code so that I/O operations are done in a separate thread from the main thread. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#blocking-io)

name: apps/com.microsoft.scmx/ddb6325e1dee34693574b9b94721d266
location: Input dispatching timed out (No focused window)

annotations.title: Slow Binder call
annotations.body:
The main thread was busy doing a Binder call that was potentially slow.

The latency of a Binder call is hard to predict. It can be affected not only by the complexity of the call itself, but also by intermittent factors such as system server lock contention. You should treat them as you would treat I/O calls, and avoid, if possible, making Binder calls in the main thread. If making a Binder call from the main thread is unavoidable, make sure to instrument and monitor such calls to detect slowness. (https://developer.android.com/topic/performance/anrs/find-unresponsive-thread#slow-binder)
